On Saturday August 17th, 2024, sometime between 2 and 4 in the morning, a vehicle drove into the chain-link fence on the west side of the Lincoln Park community garden at 312 Park Boulevard. I am the garden coordinator and I arrived at 6:30 AM Saturday for the weekly volunteer day. About sixty feet of fencing was knocked flat. Two of the raised beds inside the fence were crushed under tire marks and approximately twelve tomato plants and a small pear tree were destroyed. There were no people injured because the garden was closed at that hour. I found a broken side mirror and a piece of red plastic trim that I left in place for the police. The Lincoln Park police took a report at 8:15 AM. I am submitting this so the city has a record because the garden is partially funded by the parks department.
